meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Scale Your Freelance Workflow Faster
content.seo_description: Stop building from scratch. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance business and ship projects in days.
content.answer_block: To scale your freelance projects, stop reinventing the wheel. Use a production-ready SaaS starter kit to handle authentication, database schema, and payment integration out of the box. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you reduce development time by 80%, allowing you to ship high-quality SaaS products faster.
content.intro:
The bottleneck isn't your coding ability; it's the repetitive, soul-crushing setup of every new project. You’ve spent countless hours configuring Auth, setting up ORMs, and wrestling with Stripe integration while your clients wait for progress. This cycle of 'starting from zero' kills your profit margins and prevents you from scaling your freelance business into a true agency.

But there is a better way. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from artisan hand-crafting to industrialized shipping. We are moving past the era of manual boilerplate. With the right architecture and an AI-driven terminal, you can reclaim your time and build consistent, high-value assets for every client.

steps.title: Define Your Boilerplate Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundational stack. We prioritize speed and security by defining a robust structure that handles multi-tenant database logic and secure user sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-performance Next.js 15 project architecture with TypeScript, Drizzle ORM, and Resend for transactional emails. Focus on a modular directory structure that enforces separation of concerns for scalable SaaS deployment.

steps.title: Automate Authentication Integration
steps.description: Never write a login flow from scratch again. Implement a pre-built Auth provider that handles magic links, social logins, and secure session management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth.js with Google and GitHub providers. Ensure the user schema is automatically extended in the Drizzle database schema.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Payments are the most error-prone part of SaaS. Use a pre-tested flow to handle subscriptions, webhooks, and billing portals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription service layer. Include webhooks for 'customer.subscription.updated' and 'invoice.payment_succeeded' to sync with our database.

steps.title: Configure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
steps.description: Scalable SaaS needs fine-grained permissions. We implement middleware-based access control to protect API routes and UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a robust RBAC middleware that restricts access based on user subscription tiers and custom 'admin' or 'member' roles.

steps.title: Deploy to Production Pipeline
steps.description: Ship your project with CI/CD automation. We ensure the environment variables and secrets are handled securely for immediate deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and production deployment to Vercel.

pro_tips: Always use 'feature flags' in your starter kit to toggle complex modules like AI chat or advanced reporting without breaking the core build.
Standardize your database naming conventions across all projects so you can reuse your SQL query snippets without modification.
Keep your 'node_modules' and 'dist' folders excluded from your starter repo to prevent bloated git history.
Use a dedicated 'seed' script in your starter kit to populate your local database with mock client data for instant previewing.
Prioritize 'UI-agnostic' backend logic so you can swap out Tailwind themes for different clients while keeping the business logic identical.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is refined, document your own 'Standard Operating Procedure' (SOP) for new client onboarding. Create a private repository for your perfected boilerplate and update it every time you encounter a new edge case.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on repetitive setup and start charging for the high-level value you provide. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
